rulings.Case Title: 1429 GRANT AVE, LLC, VS. ANDREW LINTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Set Aside Dismissal, Return The Matter To The Civil Active List, And For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint [Mandatory And/Or Discretionary Relief Under Ccp 473(B)]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 27, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT ANDREW LINTON MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Set Aside Dismissal, Return The Matter To The Civil Active List, And For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint [Mandatory And/Or Discretionary Relief Under Ccp 473(B)] is DENIED. Moving party fails to establish a basis for mandatory or discretionary relief on the merits per CCP 473(b) (also see is Basinger v. Rogers & Wells (1990) 220 Cal.App.3rd 16, 269) Additionally, moving party failed to provide a copy of the proposed cross-complaint with the moving papers depriving Plaintiff of an opportunity to address it in opposition. Defendant's contention that inclusion of the proposed cross-complaint with the reply papers satisfied the procedural requirements of CCP 473(b) is not supported by any authority. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial notice is granted. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ZEUS LIVING, INC. VS. ENAS BIRAWI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion; Motion For Consideration Of Order Denying Plaintiff'S Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Affidavit Of Jane Creason; [Proposed] Order.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 27, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFF ZEUS LIVING, INC. Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion; Motion For Consideration Of Order Denying Plaintiff'S Motion For Summary Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Affidavit Of Jane Creason; [Proposed] Order. is DENIED. On May 22, 2023 the Court issued its tentative ruling reflecting the rules under CCP 1170.9 and CRC 3.1351(b)-(c) allowing for an opposition to be presented at the hearing or in writing by the end of the day, i.e. "Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED, subject to opposition." At the time the Court issued its tentative ruling there was no written opposition on file. (See the Register of Actions; CRC 3.1351(c); hence no opposition could have been considered prior to the issuance of the tentative ruling as the Court was only in possession of the moving papers.) Plaintiff cites not authority, and the Court is not aware of any, for the proposition that the defendant was required to provide a notice of contesting the tentative ruling which expressly allowed the defendant to present their opposition: "subject to opposition." Plaintiff's counsel does not deny that the tentative ruling was posted and was viewable to the parties. Plaintiff's counsel does not explain why Plaintiff did not appear at the hearing to be able to participate in the hearing if and when a statutorily permitted opposition is orally presented to the Court or address any opposition that could have been filed after 3:00 p.m. the day before the hearing per CRC 3.1351(b)-(c). Plaintiff's counsel further does not indicate that Plaintiff provided any notice that they intended to contest the Court's clear indication that an opposition may be and will be considered after the tentative ruling was issued. The Court notes that neither CCP 1170.9 nor CRC 3.1351(b)-(c) is discussed in the moving papers. Plaintiff had an opportunity to participate in the hearing and Plaintiff did not do so. Plaintiff had notice of the hearing (having calendared the motion) and notice of the Court's intent to consider any opposition presented to the Court. Therefore, Plaintiff's decision not to appear at the hearing does or the Court's entertaining a statutorily permitted opposition (of which Plaintiff was informed in advance) does not constitute a new fact within the meaning of CCP 1008 that could not have been raised at the hearing.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC VS. JACQUELINE GODZICH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 27, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT JACQUELINE GODZICH DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to attach the entire rental agreement, which per paragraph XVIII of the Lease consists of "this lease document and listed attachments." Plaintiff may instead specify the legally appropriate reasons for failure to attach (e.g. complaint solely for non-payment of rent, etc). Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Notice is legally sufficient. None of the authorities cited by the Defendant stand for the proposition that the operative notice must include the dates of the incidents or the specific details regarding the visitors. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
